perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree to abide by the following rules of non-circumvention and non-disclosure for a period of Two years from the effective date hereof. Such covenant and agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree to abide by the following rules of non-circumvention and non-disclosure for a period of One year from the effective date hereof. Such covenant and agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement only for breaches that occurred during the one-year period.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the non-circumvention and non-disclosure agreement. Section 1 now states the agreement lasts for one year, while Section 2 later states that the agreement is valid for two years. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the enforceable duration of the non-circumvention and non-disclosure obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Agreement is valid and effective for all purposes, business, communications, negotiations, disclosures and transactions of whatever nature between the Parties for a period of two (2) years from the effective date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of the non-circumvention and non-disclosure agreement from two years to one year. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the agreement is valid for two years. This creates a clear and impactful contradiction within the document as it pertains to the duration of the agreement, falling under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "associates" or "contacts" shall be defined as: in the case of a business entity its officers, directors, affiliates, subsidiaries, associated entities, and any other business entity in which the business entity owns five percent (5%) or more of the outstanding equity interest.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "associates" or "contacts" shall be defined as: in the case of a business entity its officers, directors, affiliates, subsidiaries, associated entities, and any other business entity in which the business entity owns fifteen percent (15%) or more of the outstanding equity interest.
perturbation.explanation: The change of ownership percentage from 5% to 15% creates an in-text contradiction. Some clauses referring to the definition of 'associates' and 'contacts' may imply that any entity with 5% ownership is included, while this modified clause defines it as 15%. This leads to confusion as to which standard should be used when identifying associates and contacts, particularly in clause 1.a, where engaging with an associate without permission is prohibited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a) Each Party, for itself and its associates as defined below, represents and warrants that it shall not conduct business with any sources or contacts, or said source's or contact's associates as defined below, that are originally made known and/or available by another Party hereto, at any time or in any manner, without the express written permission (not to be unreasonably withheld) of the Party who made the source(s) known and/or available.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'associates' or 'contacts' by increasing the ownership percentage required to qualify as such from 5% to 15%. The contradicted text, clause a), prohibits conducting business with associates without permission. Because the definition of associates is directly altered, and the contradicted text relies on that definition, a clear contradiction arises. Before the change, an entity with 5% ownership would be considered an associate under clause a). After the change, that same entity would no longer be considered an associate under the modified definition. This creates an inconsistency because it's unclear whether conducting business with an entity that owns between 5% and 15% requires permission or not, directly impacting the obligations outlined in clause a).

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be modified only by written agreement duly executed by all Parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified by email confirmation from all parties or by written agreement duly executed by all Parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of section 6, which now states that the agreement can be modified by email, introduces an in-text contradiction with section 9, which gives the emailed agreement the same weight as the original hand delivered copy. This creates uncertainty on whether this email agreement applies to the modification of the contract as stated in section 6 or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Any copy of this Agreement, or any other documents executed and/or signed by any of the Parties hereto, and sent to another Party hereto by facsimile transmission carries the full force and effect as if it were the hand delivered original.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6 introduces the possibility of modifying the agreement via email confirmation, which directly contradicts section 9, which states that agreements sent by facsimile have the same weight as hand-delivered originals. The contradiction arises because it is now unclear whether email agreements are also considered to have the same weight as originals, particularly regarding contract modifications, creating uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be adjudicated in arbitration under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The prevailing party in any dispute shall be reimbursed reasonable attorneys fees.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be adjudicated in court under the laws of Pennsylvania. The prevailing party in any dispute shall not be reimbursed attorney's fees.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct conflict with the original clause regarding dispute resolution. It now mandates court adjudication instead of arbitration, contradicting the established arbitration clause. Moreover, it reverses the fee reimbursement policy, leading to uncertainty in case of disputes. This contradicts the prior half of the clause itself and introduces ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by law and construed to be in accordance with the laws of the State of Pennsylvania applicable to contracts made and to be performed solely in such State by parties thereof. Any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be adjudicated in court under the laws of Pennsylvania. The prevailing party in any dispute shall not be reimbursed attorney's fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original clause by mandating court adjudication instead of arbitration, and reversing the fee reimbursement policy, leading to uncertainty and ambiguity. The contradicted text, which may be in the same location as the Changed Text, supports that the changes causes a contradiction. The change is not minor, and it impacts understanding and enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement is valid and effective for all purposes, business, communications, negotiations, disclosures and transactions of whatever nature between the Parties for a period of two (2) years from the effective date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agreement is valid and effective for all purposes, business, communications, negotiations, disclosures and transactions of whatever nature between the Parties for a period of three (3) years from the effective date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction concerning the duration of the agreement. The body of the agreement now claims a three-year validity period. This conflicts with Section 1 where the Non-Circumvention and Non-Disclosure terms are agreed to last for only one year. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree to abide by the following rules of non-circumvention and non-disclosure for a period of One year from the effective date hereof. Such covenant and agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement only for breaches that occurred during the one-year period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the agreement's validity period from two to three years. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the non-circumvention and non-disclosure terms are only valid for one year. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document as the agreement's overall duration is now inconsistent with the duration of specific clauses.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire and complete understanding existing between the Parties of the date of its execution regarding the subject matters contained herein, and all former representations, promises or covenants, whether written or verbal, are null and void.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire and complete understanding existing between the Parties of the date of its execution regarding the subject matters contained herein, however any verbal agreement will supersede this written one, and all former written representations, promises or covenants are null and void.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text now states that verbal agreements will supersede the written agreement. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the rest of the document treats the written agreement as the primary and legally binding agreement, especially in clauses regarding modifications and dispute resolution. This introduces ambiguity over the validity of the written content of the agreement and uncertainty over whether verbal agreements will stand despite the existence of written clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be modified by email confirmation from all parties or by written agreement duly executed by all Parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states the agreement contains the entire understanding and all prior agreements are void, while the changed text says verbal agreements supersede the written agreement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states modifications must be in writing (email or executed agreement). The changed text makes the enforceability of the written agreement uncertain by prioritizing verbal agreements. Therefore, it's a strong contradiction.
